author: Yutaka Kamei
message:
Add coverage.vim to .gitignore

When I run tests on my machine, `coverage.vim` is generated because of
simplecov-vim. I think the generated file should not be managed by Git.

https://github.com/zph/simplecov-vim

author: Roger Pack
message:
Merge pull request #61 from yykamei/test

Make tests compatible with Ruby 3.2

author: Yutaka Kamei
message: Fix the target branch for push event

author: Yutaka Kamei
message:
Make tests compatible with Ruby 3.2

Previously, tests with Ruby version 3.2 didn't pass because the Bundler
version was not compatible with the latest Ruby. Therefore, I made
changes to remove the version constraint for `bundler` in gemspec.

Even though I loosened the version constraint, the tests still didn't
pass. Upon reviewing the test code, I found some issues:

* Test cases are accessing private methods without using `#send`.
* One test case assumes that the `*.gem` file exists.

With this patch, I aim to ensure that specific_install_spec can be
tested with ease. Additionally, I want to suggest adding a GitHub
workflow to ensure tests are run whenever pull requests are submitted.

author: Roger Pack
message:
Merge pull request #52 from jubishop/master

This successfully swallows the error:

author: Justin Bishop
message:
This successfully swallows the error:

Error loading RubyGems plugin "/Users/jubishop/.rbenv/versions/3.0.0/lib/ruby/gems/3.0.0/plugins/specific_install_plugin.rb": cannot load such file -- specific_install/version (LoadError)

which arises when running `bundle exec gem build <gemspec>` anywhere.  But general `gem specific_install` functionality remains and works great.

author: Roger Pack
message:
Merge pull request #51 from the-unbelievable-machine/develop

[check git] use shell built-in "type -a" to search for "git" executable

author: lush
message:
[check git] use shell built-in "type" to search for "git"

Use shell built-in "type -a", instead of "which", "where" or "whereis"
as well to check if "git" binary is in $PATH and installed.

In this case, "type" is used with the "-a" option, which returns all of
the places containing the executable file. Furthermore it will also get
functions and aliases.
